segments.title: French destroyers grew within infrastructure limits
segments.summary: The conclusion argues that French destroyer development stayed small and gradual because infrastructure and available yards shaped what could be built.
segments.body: France could have pursued larger destroyers earlier, but doing so would have reduced the facilities it could use and likely lowered numbers. The transcript ties that constraint to gradual hull growth, Jeune Ecole legacy, and ordinary destroyer roles of screening plus flotilla attack and defense.

evidence.note: The transcript connects limited destroyer numbers and later design ambitions to battleship construction, World War I disruption, and Jeune Ecole infrastructure legacy.
The conclusion asks whether France should have built bigger destroyers earlier, while noting the tradeoff with available facilities and numbers.
The transcript summarizes French destroyer tactics as screening plus flotilla attack and defense, with infrastructure as the limiting factor.
